Another reason I recommend VSCode for writing posts is an extension called "markdownlint". If you're a frontend dev, seeing "lint" probably makes you think of ESLint.

I personally want my articles to have a consistent style — trailing blank line at the end, consistent heading hierarchy, etc. markdownlint catches those, and reminds you of good habits like adding the language to code blocks.
(After installing this, I don't really want to look at my older posts — they're full of yellow squiggles...)
Last one — a simple feature. Sometimes you don't want to fire up
just to see how the article's coming along, but you still want a live preview. Click the arrow indicator in the image:
Once you click it, the live preview opens up on the right side of VSCode.
